specific_location: This bird was seen in the grassy wetland area just West of Logan County Rd. 95 at the first parking area for the Red Lion SWA North of U.S. highway 138.
description: The overall shape, size and color of this bird was similar to the accompanying White-faced Ibis. The Glossy Ibis was slightly larger than the others, standing about 2 ft. tall. This was clearly visible when the birds were side by side. Bill: Long, thin and decurved. Grayish in color. Face: Facial skin and lores gray bordered by light blue skin not meeting behind the eye. Eye: Brown Legs: Long and gray with reddish ankle joints. Upper parts: Head, neck and back warm chestnut brown with a glossy iridescence to the lower back. Under parts: Throat, breast and belly warm chestnut. Wings: Mostly metallic green/bronze.Behaviors: All three birds were foraging by probing into the grasses or shallows along the edges of the ponds. Occasionally a car would pass and they would get up and fly to another part of the pond. Flight was with feet and neck extended.Call: Not heardPlumage: Adult Alternate
